- [x] #822 — partial (see status below)
- [ ] #823
- [x] #824

- [x] #827

- [x] #836
- [x] #837

- [ ] #1092 — Request Problem Information (gates #823/#830)

The ones that require changes on persistences:

- [ ] #839 — partial (in-memory only; no cross-restart durability)
- [x] #841 — single-instance only (not cluster-safe)
- [x] #840 — inbound only (topic alias is per-connection; doesn't actually need persistence)

---

## Development status — `mqttv5` branch / PR #1088

Most of MQTT 5.0 has **landed on the `mqttv5` branch**: **#1088** (`feat/mqtt5`) was merged on 2026-06-30 (merge commit `e7d0ce9`) after eight rounds of review. MQTT 3.1/3.1.1 behaviour is unchanged. Tests live in `test/mqtt5.js` (40 tests, driving a real `mqtt@5` client over TCP). Note: #1088 targeted `mqttv5`, not the default branch, so the per-feature issues it referenced are **not** auto-closed — the checklist above tracks their status until `mqttv5` merges to release under #194.

**Companion releases (resolved):** the gating packages are published and `package.json` now points at published ranges — `aedes-packet@^4.0.1` (moscajs/aedes-packet#192 — preserve `properties`/`messageExpiry`) and `aedes-persistence@^10.3.1` (moscajs/aedes-persistence#108 — persist `subscriptionIdentifier`). The `overrides` shim has been removed; the `scripts/check-no-git-deps.js` release guard is no longer blocked (no git deps remain).

### Persistence & cluster classification

The features split three ways by what they touch beyond the broker core:

**A. Need `aedes-packet` changes** (the packet object that travels through the emitter + persistence must preserve the new fields): property pass-through — #826, #827, #830 — and Message Expiry #824 (`messageExpiry` must survive storage/forwarding). Covered by moscajs/aedes-packet#192.

**B. Need `aedes-persistence` changes** (and round-trip support in the `mongodb` / `redis` / `level` implementations):
- #828 Subscription Identifier — must be stored per-subscription and round-tripped by every backend (moscajs/aedes-persistence#108; backends already store the whole packet, so a conformance test rather than backend code is expected).
- #839 Session Expiry — needs a **persisted expiry deadline** + a restart-time reaper. **Not done** — currently in-memory timers only.
- #841 Shared Subscriptions — needs `addSharedSubscription`/group-membership storage; cluster-correct delivery additionally needs *global* group state in `mongodb`/`redis`.

**C. No persistence change — broker core / per-connection:** #822, #823, #825, #829, #831, #832, #833, #834, #836, #837, #838, #840.

**Special cluster handling required:**
- **#841 Shared subscriptions** — group membership is per-instance, so a shared message is delivered *once per instance* holding a group member instead of once cluster-wide. Cluster-correct delivery needs global membership + deterministic recipient selection coordinated via the persistence/emitter layer (`mqemitter-redis`/`-mongodb`).
- **#839 Session Expiry & #835 Will Delay** — enforced by per-instance in-memory timers; not durable across a broker restart or coordinated across the cluster (needs persisted deadlines + a reaper).
- Everything else is cluster-safe as-is: per-connection features (#831/#836/#837/#840/#825/#829/#833/#838, reason codes) are local to the owning instance; property forwarding (#826/#827/#830), Subscription Identifiers (#828, attached at delivery by the subscribing instance) and message/retained expiry (#824, absolute timestamps carried on the packet) ride the shared emitter + persistence correctly.

### Conformance review (vs. [OASIS MQTT 5.0](https://docs.oasis-open.org/mqtt/mqtt/v5.0/mqtt-v5.0.html))

Status: ✅ implemented & conformant · 🟡 partial · ❌ missing · ⚠️ non-conformant.
Persistence: **pkt** = needs `aedes-packet` · **persist** = needs `aedes-persistence` (+ mongodb/redis/level) · **—** = core only.
Cluster: ✅ cluster-safe · ⚠️ per-instance only / needs care · ❌ not cluster-safe.

| # | Feature | Status | Persistence | Cluster | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| #822 | Reason code on ACKs | 🟡 | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (optional — `0x80`/absent codes are legal). CONNACK/SUBACK/UNSUBACK/server-DISCONNECT carry codes. PUBREL/PUBCOMP none; PUBACK/PUBREC only `0x87`; SUBACK failure uses coarse `0x80`. |
| #823 | Reason String on ACKs | ❌ | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (optional). No `reasonString` on any ACK. |
| #824 | Message Expiry Interval | ✅ | pkt | ✅ | Recomputed/dropped on queued + retained delivery; will-msg expiry ok. *Minor:* not decremented on the live path (wait ≈ 0). |
| #825 | Server disconnect | ✅ | — | ✅ | `0x8E`/`0x8B`/`0x95`/`0x94`/`0x82`, v5-only; inbound `0x04` publishes will, `0x00` discards. |
| #826 | Payload format + content type | ✅ | pkt | ✅ | Forwarded transparently. |
| #827 | Request / Response | ✅ | pkt | ✅ | Response Topic + Correlation Data forwarded; Response Topic wildcard rejected ([MQTT-3.3.2-14]). |
| #828 | Subscription Identifier | 🟡 | persist | ✅ | Validated/persisted/echoed for single match; retained-on-subscribe now echoes the SI. **Multi-match still delivers only one SI** ([MQTT-3.3.4-4]) — deferred, documented limitation. |
| #829 | Flow control (Receive Maximum) | 🟡 | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (invasive to delivery hot path; advisory documented). Parsed (default 65535), `0` rejected, advertised. **Outbound in-flight not capped** (§4.9). |
| #830 | User Properties | ✅ | pkt | ✅ | Forwarded; preserved on the SI-merge fan-out. |
| #831 | Maximum Packet Size | ✅ | — | ✅ | Advertised; inbound oversized rejected pre-buffer (`0x95`). Outbound size-split advisory. |
| #832 | Optional feature availability | ✅ | — | ✅ | Max QoS/Retain/Wildcard/Sub-ID advertised correctly. Shared-sub flag → see #841. |
| #833 | Enhanced authentication | 🟡 | — | ✅ | CONNECT Authentication Method now rejected with `0x8C` (§4.12). **⏭️ defer** full AUTH packet flow (re-auth `0x18`/`0x19`). |
| #834 | Subscription options | ✅ | — | ✅ | Max QoS ✅, No Local ✅, Retain As Published ✅, Retain Handling 0/1/2 ✅ ([MQTT-3.8.3-2]). |
| #835 | Will delay | ✅ | persist | ⚠️ | Will persisted (no schema change); reconnect cancels. Delay timer is per-instance (cross-broker will-sweep partially covers). |
| #836 | Server Keep Alive | ✅ | — | ✅ | Imposed when unset/over-limit; echoed in CONNACK. |
| #837 | Assigned Client Identifier | ✅ | — | ✅ | Generated + echoed only on empty client id. |
| #838 | Server reference | ❌ | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (new feature; needs redirect API). No `serverReference`, no `0x9C`/`0x9D`. |
| #839 | Session Expiry Interval | 🟡 | persist ❌ | ⚠️ | Correct within a running broker; inbound-DISCONNECT expiry validation now enforced ([MQTT-3.14.4-3]). **⏭️ defer** cross-restart persistence (needs schema + reaper) — in-memory timers only. |
| #840 | Topic Alias | 🟡 | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (outbound is optional / MAY). **Inbound ✅** (resolve/register/strip/alias-0 + over-max reject, per-connection reset). **Outbound ❌** (broker-assigned). |
| #841 | Shared Subscriptions | 🟡 | persist | ❌ | `$share` SUBSCRIBE now refused with `0x9E` ([MQTT-3.2.2-23]). **⏭️ defer** cluster-aware feature; single-instance impl on the `sharedsubs` branch (persistence-backed, not cluster-safe). #1088 advertises `sharedSubscriptionAvailable: false`. |
| #1091 | Request / Response Information | ❌ | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (optional — server MAY, §3.2.2.3.15). CONNECT `0x19` → CONNACK `0x1A` not handled. Distinct from #827 (per-message Response Topic) and #832 (capability flags). |
| #1092 | Request Problem Information | ❌ | — | ✅ | **⏭️ defer** (benign until #823/#830 land). CONNECT `0x17` ([MQTT-3.1.2-29]) gates Reason String / User Properties on non-PUBLISH/CONNACK/DISCONNECT packets — nothing to suppress today. |

### Triage — complete in #1088 vs. defer

**Principle:** fix in #1088 anything where the PR already *advertises or accepts* a capability but then *mishandles* it (spec violation / misleading behavior). Defer net-new scope, persistence-schema, and cluster work. Legend: 🔧 #1088 · 🤔 judgment · ⏭️ defer.

**✅ Completed in #1088** (landed in `ceed520`, verified against source):

1. **Retain Handling 1/2 (#834)** — [MQTT-3.8.3-2]. `rh=2` no longer sends retained on subscribe; `rh=1` only for a brand-new subscription (`subscribe.js`).
2. **`$share` → `0x9E` rejection (#841)** — [MQTT-3.2.2-23]. A v5 `$share/...` SUBSCRIBE is refused with `0x9E` instead of becoming a dead literal subscription.
3. **Inbound DISCONNECT Session-Expiry validation (#825/#839)** — [MQTT-3.14.4-3]. A non-zero Session Expiry on DISCONNECT is rejected when CONNECT declared `0`.
4. **Reject unsupported Authentication Method `0x8C` (#833 guard)** — §4.12. A CONNECT carrying `authenticationMethod` is rejected with `0x8C` instead of a silent password downgrade.
5. **Retained-on-subscribe SI echo (#828, part)** — [MQTT-3.3.4]. Retained deliveries now carry the SUBSCRIBE Subscription Identifier.
6. **Response Topic wildcard validation** — [MQTT-3.3.2-14]. A PUBLISH with a wildcard Response Topic is rejected.

**🤔 Judgment call:**

- **Multiple matching SIs collapsed to one (#828)** — [MQTT-3.3.4-4]. Strictly, one delivery should carry *all* matching subscription identifiers; `dedupe()` drops all but one. The fix reaches into the dedupe/delivery core (medium effort, real regression surface, low real-world hit rate). **Lean: defer with a documented limitation + dedicated issue**, unless strict conformance is wanted now.

**⏭️ Defer to follow-ups (net-new scope / persistence / cluster / spec-optional):**

- **#841 full cluster-aware shared subscriptions** — `sharedsubs` rewrite + global group state in `mqemitter-redis`/`-mongodb`.
- **#839 cross-restart session-expiry persistence** — schema + mongodb/redis/level round-trip + reaper.
- **#829 outbound Receive Maximum enforcement** — invasive to the shared delivery hot path; advisory-only documented.
- **#833 full enhanced auth (AUTH packet flow)**.
- **#838 Server reference** — new feature; needs redirect API design.
- **#823 Reason String on ACKs**, **#822 granular ACK reason codes**, **#840 outbound Topic Alias** — all spec-*optional*.
- **#1091 Request / Response Information**, **#1092 Request Problem Information** — net-new optional properties (spec-completeness pass); #1092 also gates #823/#830 once those land.

> Status generated from a spec-conformance audit of `mqttv5` @ PR #1088 head (`276cb2e`). Shared-subscription code currently lives on the separate `sharedsubs` branch (single-instance, pre-v5). Items marked 🟡/❌/⚠️ are the remaining work toward full v5 conformance (#194).
